summary: President Eyring teaches that continual, heartfelt prayer invites the Savior’s promised peace and strengthening support in every circumstance.
haiku:
Hearts rise silently
Peace descends through faithful prayer
Christ calms troubled souls
key_points: The Savior offers peace that is deeper and more lasting than anything the world can give.
Prayer need not be eloquent or lengthy; God hears sincere prayers from the heart, even unspoken ones.
Consistent prayer in both joy and hardship brings spiritual strength, revelation, and the ability to bear burdens.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: John 14:27 — “Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you.”
Psalm 9:9–10 — The Lord is a refuge in times of trouble.
Matthew 6:6–8 — Pray sincerely, not with vain repetitions.
Mosiah 24:12–15 — Alma’s people pray in their hearts and receive strength to bear burdens.
Alma 17:3 — The sons of Mosiah receive spiritual power through prayer and fasting.
Alma 7:27 — The peace of God resting upon homes and families.
Principle of praying always and having hearts drawn out continually to God.
questions_to_ponder: How have you felt the Savior’s peace in answer to prayer?
What does it mean to have a continual prayer in your heart?
How can sincere prayer help us when burdens are not immediately removed?
Why is it important to pray consistently before moments of crisis arise?
How can we make personal and family prayers more heartfelt?
